title: camp index entry
description: One plugin's authoritative record in the camp index (RFC §4.1). Descriptive listing content lives in the plugin repo (.camp/listing.yml); this file holds only what the registry itself asserts or arbitrates: name ownership, tier, and the append-only release ledger.

component.description: Frankenstyle component name; the registry is the arbiter of this mapping (RFC §8).

source.description: Canonical public source repository. All releases must be buildable from tags in this repository.

security-contact.description: Email or private vulnerability-reporting URL (RFC §5.1). CI validates presence; preference is the source platform's private reporting channel.

tier.description: Trust tier (RFC §4.4). 0 = discovered (metadata only), 1 = claimed (author accountable, no verified releases yet), 2 = source-verified, 3 = human-reviewed. Releases may only be listed from tier 2 up.

labels.description: Disclosure labels (RFC §4.7), mirrored from the listing manifest at the latest release and validated by CI heuristics / Tier 3 review.

status.description: Listing status. 'delisted' entries remain for the archive but are not served for installation. 'moved' entries now publish elsewhere (see moved-to): published versions stay installable and advisory-covered, but no new releases land here.

moved-to.description: Where the plugin now publishes (a URL, or a Composer package name): the successor pointer for status 'moved' (RFC §6.3), surfaced by clients and emitted as Composer's 'abandoned' replacement so composer warns natively.

summary.description: Short description for Tier 0 discovered listings (taken from the source platform's repo description). From Tier 1 up, descriptive content comes from the plugin's own .camp/listing.yml instead.

discovered.description: Date the ecosystem seeding scan first found this plugin (Tier 0 provenance).

metrics.description: Upstream repository activity signals, refreshed periodically by `camp enrich` from the source platform's API (GitHub/GitLab). Advisory only — they help prioritize which discovered plugins to verify next; they are not registry assertions and carry a `checked` date so consumers can judge freshness.

updated.description: Upstream last-activity timestamp (GitHub pushed_at / GitLab last_activity_at). The maintenance-liveness signal.

open-issues.description: Open issues (on GitHub this also counts open pull requests, per the API).

archived.description: True when the maintainer has archived the source repository (upstream is frozen/dead).

checked.description: Date these metrics were last fetched from the source platform.

latest-release.description: Upstream's newest published release (GitHub releases / GitLab releases), refreshed by `camp enrich`. Advisory display data — distinct from the registry's own verified release ledger.

license.description: Detected SPDX license identifier. GPL-family is the ecosystem norm; GPL-compatible permissive licenses are listed with the license surfaced as a badge (disclosure, not gatekeeping).

releases.description: Append-only release ledger (RFC §4.2). Entries are never modified or removed once merged; CI enforces this on every pull request. Revocation is expressed by a signed advisory (RFC §5.3), not by editing history.

tag.description: Git tag in the source repository this release was built from.

commit.description: Commit the tag pointed to at publication. If the tag later moves, verification fails and the maintainer is alerted (RFC §4.2).

supported-moodle.description: Moodle branches this release supports, e.g. "4.3".

php-min.description: $plugin->php at the tagged commit, if declared.

zip-sha256.description: SHA-256 of the canonical distribution ZIP built deterministically from the tagged commit (see tools/camp/build.py for the canonical form).

listing-sha256.description: SHA-256 of .camp/listing.yml at the tagged commit, pinning the descriptive listing at release (RFC §4.1). Absent when the repo has no manifest yet.

published.description: UTC timestamp the release was merged into the index. Basis for cooldown policies (RFC §4.4).

released.description: UTC timestamp the maintainer released this version — the committer date of the tagged commit. This is the user-facing "last updated" date; distinct from `published`, which is when CAMP merged it into the index. Absent on entries recorded before this field existed; consumers fall back to `published`.

allOf.description: Below Tier 2 there is no verified artifact: discovered (0) and claimed (1) listings carry no releases. A release enters the ledger only by passing verification, which is what makes the plugin tier 2.
From Tier 1 up (claimed listings), the author must declare disclosure labels and a security contact (RFC §4.7, §5.1). Tier 0 discovered listings have neither yet.
A moved listing must say where the plugin went; the pointer is the whole point of the status.
